name: Zambia Bata Shoe Company PLC
description: Zambia Bata Shoe Company PLC is a subsidiary of the global Bata Shoe Organization, one of the world's largest footwear manufacturers and retailers. Listed on the LuSE Main Board since 1990, BATA manufactures and retails footwear through a network of company-owned stores and wholesale channels across Zambia. The company employs approximately 94 people.

analystNotes.note: BATA is a steady, unspectacular business: 18.6% revenue growth to ZMW 352M, 48.7% gross margins, zero debt, and modest profitability (9.5% net margin). The 1.67% dividend yield is negligible. At PE 20.5x, the stock is not cheap for a small, low-growth footwear company. BATA's investment case would require significant revenue acceleration or margin expansion that isn't visible in the current numbers. The 45.4% 52-week price appreciation may have already priced in the recovery. Decent business, wrong price.
analystNotes.author: Vituli Musukuma, PhD

riskFactors: Small scale — ZMW 352M revenue limits growth investment capacity
Import competition and informal sector footwear
Dividend yield too low to compensate for illiquidity
investmentThesis: BATA is a conservatively-run business with zero debt, decent margins, and a global parent. But at 20.5x earnings for a ZMW 352M revenue company with 9.5% net margins, there's no margin of safety. The 1.67% dividend yield doesn't compensate for LuSE liquidity risk. A good company, but overpriced relative to alternatives like ZNCO (PE 8.2x, 5.8% yield).
dataSources: BATA FY2024 Abridged Financial Statements (via AfricanFinancials, LuSE SENS)
dataQuality: HIGH — Based on audited FY2024 annual report with FY2025 TTM data from StockAnalysis
